title: Cashew nut yields
subtitle: Yields are measured in tonnes per hectare.
caption:
Cashew nut yields

Yields are measured in tonnes per hectare.

The line chart tracks cashew nut yields in tonnes per hectare for Peru, Mali, China, and Mexico from 1980 to 2024. Peru’s data, shown in brown, begins around 1988 near 5.3 tonnes per hectare and fluctuates between approximately 3.7 and 6.8 tonnes per hectare through 2024, with peaks near 6.8 around 2005 and 2021, and a low near 3.7 in 2019. Mali’s green line starts in 1992 at about 1.3 tonnes per hectare, remains flat until 2005, then rises gradually to 1.7 by 2010, accelerates after 2015 to reach 4.2 in 2019, drops sharply to 1.8 in 2021, and ends near 1.1 in 2024. China’s red line begins in 1985 below 0.5 tonnes per hectare, spikes to 1.5 in 1989, falls back below 0.5 by 1992, stays low until 2008, climbs to 1.8 in 2013, dips to 0.9 in 2015, and stabilizes near 1.1 from 2018 to 2024. Mexico’s blue line starts at 1.1 in 1980, surges to 6.1 in 1983, declines to 2.5 by 1986, fluctuates between 0.1 and 3.5 until 2015, then falls steadily to below 0.8 by 2024. All four countries show distinct patterns of volatility and stability across the period, with Peru consistently yielding above 3.5 tonnes per hectare after 1988, while the others remain mostly below 2.0 except during brief upward spikes.
